Pack identity, risk class, unit count, range, and the deliverable/phase/micro-phase/micro-title distributions reconcile exactly across the pack binding (registry.js EMAIL_DMS_CORE_CP286_PACK_BINDING), review-baseline.json, manifest.json, the live closeout-pack-plan.json entry, and the contract (current_pack, program.current_pack_id, fixture_slice_descriptor.pack_binding, and historical_pack_bindings tail), summing to 10 with distribution {implementation:3, security_audit:2, fixture:1, test:3, hermes_evidence:1}. The handoff chain is continuous and gap-free: CP00-285.next_subphase=RP08.P05.M03.S09=CP00-286.first_unit, and CP00-286 hands off to CP00-287 at RP08.P05.M03.S19. All 7 required public exports exist (createEmailDmsCoreCp286FixtureSliceCaseSet/Descriptor in service.js; validateEmailDmsCoreCp286Coverage/FixtureSliceDescriptor and createEmailDmsCoreCp286HermesEvidencePacket/ClaudeReviewPacket/CloseoutHandoff in validators.js), are surfaced via index.js export-*, and are imported and exercised by both model.test.js and the validate script. The CP286 validators enforce coverage drift (id/risk/count/first/last/duplicate/program-only/deliverable/phase/micro-phase/micro-title/section-row checks), descriptor-only + runtime_execution=false + customer_safe + raw_payload=false row guards, the per-row security guards (cross_tenant_access_allowed=false, audit_hint_detail_included=false, unauthorized_data_omitted=true, dispatches_ai_runtime=false, fixture_manifest_descriptor_only=true, executes_unit_test_runtime_paths=false for golden/failure tests, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, claude_final_approval_claimed=false), the authority boundary, contract-projection drift, and CP00-287/S19 handoff routing; I confirmed the backing row-extras properties (inherited via EMAIL_DMS_CORE_CP285_ROW_EXTRAS spread) are present, so the guards are enforced rather than vacuous. Strong anti-drift exists: validate-rp08-email-dms-core-contract.mjs deep-equals the contract JSON (fixture_slice_descriptor, cp286_requirements, cp286_no_write_attestation) against freshly computed source constants, and model.test.js asserts coverage.valid, slice.valid, descriptor-only rows, the security guards, and the authority boundary. The production_ready claim is appropriately conservative: manifest.json and command-evidence.json declare production_ready=false with only production_ready_candidate=true derived from local coverage+slice validity, explicitly gated behind human final approval; no IO/runtime imports exist anywhere in packages/email-dms/src (no fs/http/https/child_process/net/dns/process.env/fetch/require), all result objects are Object.freeze'd, no_real_data=true, and Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic H08 evidence/receipt shapes (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false everywhere; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false). Review-hardening is satisfied: forbidden_review_evidence covers every CP178+ rejection case (failed_authentication_run, zero_byte_output, malformed_json_output, fenced_json_output, tool_call_shaped_output, disabled_tools_run, missing_raw_output_reference, missing_source_inspection_basis, claude_final_approval_claim, source_mutation_claim, production_enterprise_trust_claim), the readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1432-byte valid JSON), tools are restricted to Read/Grep/Glob, and post-review evidence files (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are correctly absent because this review gates them. All pre-review validation commands in command-evidence.json exit 0 (closeout-pack-plan:validate, rp05:matter-core:validate, rp06/rp07/rp08 validates, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, build, git diff --check); closeout-pack:validate is correctly deferred as a post-review gated command. Changed-file scope is contained to the email-dms package plus the contract and validate script (7 modified tracked files) with no scope creep, and unrelated dirty files (the v2.0 .docx and the LDIP c-ldip-01 invalid-attempt JSON) are correctly excluded from active_pack_implementation_refs. One trivial non-blocking P3: changed-file-scope.json lists this pack's own artifact dir (docs/closeout-packs/cp00-286/) and the new-session-handoff doc under both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, but the authoritative active_pack_implementation_refs correctly excludes docs so the implementation review scope, descriptor-only boundary, production_ready gating, no-real-data/no-write claims, and review-hardening determinations are all unaffected. No P0/P1/P2 issues. These are contradictory buckets: the docs/closeout-packs/cp00-286/ directory holds this pack's own manifest.json and command-evidence.json, so it is pack-related, not unrelated. This is an evidence-hygiene inconsistency only; the authoritative active_pack_implementation_refs list (lines 5-14) correctly contains just the 8 source/contract/test/script files and excludes these doc paths, so the implementation review scope, the descriptor-only boundary, the production_ready gating, the no-real-data/no-write claims, and the review-hardening determinations are all unaffected.
